title: Rajasthan heatwave warning, scorching heat in 18 cities: Jaipur records hottest day of the season; know how... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Rajasthan heatwave warning, scorching heat in 18 cities:Jaipur records hottest day of the season; know how the weather will stay next week
- Copy link
The heat in Rajasthan has now started to intensify. In Jaipur, on April 18, the temperature crossed 40 degrees Celsius for the first time this season. Apart from Jaipur, the maximum temperature in 18 cities on Saturday was recorded between 40 and 43 degrees Celsius.
The areas of Jhunjhunu and Alwar experienced heatwave conditions during the day on Saturday. The Meteorological Centre Jaipur has issued a yellow alert for intense heat and heatwave in the state for the next 3-4 days.
Clouds, and dusty winds also blew
In the last 24 hours, light clouds remained over Jaisalmer, Jodhpur, Barmer, Bikaner, and Phalodi. Apart from Sriganganagar, Hanumangarh, dusty winds blew in some places in Jaisalmer from morning till noon. Whereas in the areas of Kota, Jaipur, Bharatpur, Ajmer, and Udaipur divisions, the sky remained clear and there was strong sunshine throughout the day.
Health Department on alert due to increasing heat
Along with the desert districts, the temperature in Kota is also around 42 degrees Celsius. Here, anticipating a heatwave, the Health Department has started preparations. Considering the rising temperature and the possibility of heatstroke, the medical department is on alert mode.
Separate wards are also being set up in the hospital for patients suffering from heatstroke/heat-related illnesses. Additionally, doctors have also issued an advisory for protection against the heat.

title: Heatwave Alert in Rajasthan from April 19, Western Region to Be Worst Hit - Sangri Today
full_text:
Heatwave Alert in Rajasthan from April 19, Western Region to Be Worst Hit
Rajasthan Weather Update: Severe heat is affecting life in Rajasthan. According to the Meteorological Department, the heatwave will intensify from April 19th, with temperatures expected to reach 42 to 45 degrees Celsius.
Rajasthan is experiencing extremely hot conditions at present. The heat is posing great difficulties for the people living in the desert and disrupting their daily routines. According to the Meteorological Department, the heat will increase further in the region beginning April 19.
Between April 19 and April 22, a heatwave is expected in eastern Rajasthan, whereas western Rajasthan will experience it more intensely between April 21 and April 22. The temperatures may rise to 42 to 45 degrees Celsius. Jaipur, Kota, and Udaipur would be severely impacted by the heat, whereas Bikaner and Jaisalmer would be significantly affected. There won't be any rain, and the weather will remain dry.
In Rajasthan, Jaisalmer, Jodhpur, Barmer, Bikaner, and Phalodi experienced light clouds over the past 24 hours. Dust storms blew in some areas of Sriganganagar, Hanumangarh, and Jaisalmer from morning to afternoon. However, clear skies and bright sunshine prevailed throughout the day in Kota, Jaipur, Bharatpur, Ajmer, and Udaipur divisions.
